epigraph.text: The notion that the ordinary citizen is fit, competent, and morally authorized to rule his betters is gone glimmering at last.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Student Suspended for Misgendering Squirrel in Presentation
follyOfTheDay.summary:
In a decision that manages to misinterpret both biological nomenclature and the purpose of higher education, a major East Coast university has suspended a biology student for six weeks following what administrators deemed a grievous infraction: the casual misuse of a pronoun when referring to a rodent during a course presentation.

The squirrel in question—a specimen of Sciurus carolinensis, a species whose reproductive anatomy is presumably a matter of some indifference to the animal itself—was apparently referred to as 'he' by the student during what should have been an unremarkable slide presentation. The administration, demonstrating the sort of breathtaking literalism that once burned heretics and now merely ruins educations, determined that this violated community standards regarding respectful language.

What begins as Kafkaesque absurdity becomes genuinely tragic when one considers that this student's educational progress, professional trajectory, and psychological well-being have all been subordinated to an exercise in semantic overkill. One might have thought universities existed to cultivate wisdom and critical thinking. One would, at this point, be demonstrated a fool.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: The university has finally achieved what was once thought impossible: a solution so perfectly calibrated to the problem that neither bears any relationship to reality.
